Empires & Puzzles Bearnadette Review: 5-Star Legendary Fire Red Hero Guide

Bearnadette isn't the bear we expected, but she's the nightmare we deserved. This artistic grandma turns enemies into harmless paintings, dominating Rush Wars with a priority dispel and stats that refuse to quit. Discover why she's the current queen of the canvas.
Bearnadette: The Artistic Annihilator - Cursed Canvases & Rush War Reigns
Bearnadette, The Artistic Annihilator: Cursed Canvases and Rush War Reigns

Bearnadette is a 5-star Legendary Fire hero from the Clash of Knights event, released in July 2024. As a member of the Bear family, she brings incredible durability and a unique, game-altering mechanic to the battlefield. While her appearance might suggest a kindly grandmother with a hobby for painting, her gameplay is anything but gentle. She acts as a "game over" button in Rush formats, turning entire enemy teams into harmless works of art. However, her Very Slow mana speed serves as a significant gatekeeper to her power in other modes.

The community's reaction to Bearnadette's art was... mixed, to say the least. In an event named "Clash of Knights" featuring the "Bear" family, players eagerly anticipated a ferocious ursine warrior. Instead, they got a whimsical, elderly woman with a paintbrush and a mischievous grin. While the art style is technically proficient and full of character, it felt like a bait-and-switch for those hoping for a literal bear. She looks less like she's about to lead a charge into battle and more like she's about to critique your watercolor technique in a park.

Bearnadette boasts stats that are firmly in the modern elite tier. Her Defense (1133) and Health (2020) are colossal, allowing her to withstand significant punishment—a necessity given her Very Slow speed. Her Attack (1011) is respectable but secondary to her role as a survivalist tank. Compared to older Very Slow heroes like Alfrike or Rush tanks like Khufu, Bearnadette represents a significant leap in raw survivability, ensuring she often lives long enough to fire at least once.

The core of the skill, however, is the

ailment. It functions similarly to "Mindless Attack" but with a twist. It completely replaces the enemy hero, reducing their Attack and Defense to 70% of their original values. The enemy loses access to their own special skill and instead gains "Break Free," a Very Fast skill that deals a measly 250% damage (based on the reduced attack) to a single target. There is a 60% chance they will revert to their normal form upon firing, but until then, they are effectively neutralized.
With a level 29 Mana Troop (+15%) and the Druid talent (+4%), you reach +19%.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Bear family and her passives are tailored entirely towards keeping her alive as a tank.

This bonus is exceptionally strong for a tank. It makes her immune to standard Defense Down ailments. Instead of becoming vulnerable, she becomes tougher (up to +70% defense). This forces attackers to rely on elemental defense down or raw damage rather than standard debuffs to break her shell.

Recovering 5% health every time she receives a buff adds up quickly, especially when paired with buffers like Ludwig or healers who provide HoT and defense buffs. It adds another layer of sustain to an already thick health pool.
is the perfect Aether Power for a Very Slow tank. It provides a steady stream of overheal at the start of the match, mitigating early tile damage before your healers can fire.

Upon release, Bearnadette immediately drew comparisons to Alfrike, the queen of Rush Attack. She quickly displaced many older tanks in Rush Wars due to her survivability and the debilitating nature of her skill.
Her stats are top-tier. Her mechanic is unique and powerful. While newer heroes continue to push stat boundaries, her 2000+ base HP ensures she will remain relevant as a meat shield for a long time.

in Rush Attack tournaments and wars. Outside of Rush, she is a risky but high-reward pick. She requires specific team compositions (mana boosters) to function in standard speeds but is nearly unbeatable if she fires.
At the time of writing, Bearnadette does not have a costume.
As a tank whose primary job is to survive until she casts, there is only one logical path.
Prioritize Defense and Health nodes. Take the shield path at every junction.

path is recommended. You want her to live. Her painting does the rest.
If she fires, the match is effectively reset in your favor, with the enemy team crippled.
Removes Taunt and other defensive buffs before applying her ailment, ensuring it lands on everyone (unless resisted).
High stats, passive healing, and a family bonus that flips defense down into defense up.
One of the most oppressive tanks in the Very Fast format.
Without Rush rules or mana boosters (Ludwig), she can be dead weight.
Toons (75% resist), Monks (30% resist), and the Construct Family (resists special skill blocking) can shrug off her ailment.
Her ailment does not work on Bosses or Titans, making her useless in those modes.
The 60% "Break Free" chance means sometimes an enemy might snap out of it immediately, though they still waste mana.
This is her home. She is S+ tier here.
Viable as a tank if flanked by strong support or if the attacker lacks counters.
Useful on offense if paired with Ludwig or Xnolphod to cheat her mana speed.
Ludwig charges her. Nautica provides mana. Starwalker punishes anyone who tries to cleanse.
Ludwig flanking Bearnadette allows her to fire in standard speed raids. Once she fires, Khufu cleans up the softened targets.
The passive resistance of Toon costumes (75%) is the biggest soft counter. They often simply ignore the painting effect.
Due to a mechanics update, Constructs resist the Cursed Painting effect because it counts as "blocking a special skill."
Heroes that provide ailment immunity (if fired first) completely shut her down.
Heroes like Hel, Proteus, or Silence effects prevent her from ever firing.
He can copy her skill at Average speed and use it against her team.

If you are lucky enough to pull her early, she will carry you through lower-level raids simply by being unkillable. Focus on keeping her alive. However, don't rely on her for Map Stages or Titans, as her main trick doesn't work on bosses.

She is a luxury weapon for Rush Wars. If you care about competitive warring, she is worth the resources. However, be aware of the rising number of counters (Toons, Constructs). She may not be the "set and forget" tank forever, but for now, she demands an answer from the attacker.
The community's relationship with Bearnadette is a saga of disappointment turned to fear.
The forums were flooded with complaints about her design.
"Aw man, she’s not even a bear! Here I was all excited... but nooooooo."
Initially, the Construct family did not resist her. Later, it was changed so their core passive (resisting special skill blocking) protected them from Cursed Painting. This was met with skepticism:
"Ahh yes, the grey area of 'nerf by bug fix'. We aren’t changing the way it works, we are just fixing it!"
Despite the art complaints, players quickly realized her power.
"She is exactly as devastating as predicted for very fast tournaments."

Bearnadette is the classic "ugly duckling" who grew up to be a nuclear Godzilla. While players wanted a ferocious bear, they got an eccentric painter who erases entire teams from existence. In Rush Attack, she is an absolute terror, demanding specific counters or a very lucky board. Outside of Rush, she is a niche toy for those who like to play with mana mechanics.
If you have her, frame her. If you face her, bring a Toon and pray.
Note: This review is based on the hero's state as of January 2026. Future balance updates or new hero families may alter her standing.
